behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: this is backoff math/state progression inside one retry-policy object (failure count -> next delay, capped). PASS2 counter-argument (Integration) rejected because behavior text does not require module handoff, transport, timers, or I/O; the failure is in local calculation logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure backoff policy function/object with consecutive failure counts (1..N) and assert delays follow exponential growth and clamp at configured max.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: failure boundary is module interaction (HTTP-response classification + retry/backoff coordinator). MOCK-MISS applies because miswiring between classifier and scheduler can pass isolated unit tests. PASS2 counter-argument (System) rejected: behavior text does not require real network/OS effects; synthetic HTTP responses reproduce the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry coordinator with a fake transport response object (e.g., 429 + Retry-After / quota headers) and assert it flags retryable quota exhaustion and produces expected next-wait duration.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration retained after System brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: this validates orchestration across retry loop, timer abstraction, and transport adapter. Unit is too low because resend of original request is a cross-module handoff. PASS2 counter-argument (System) rejected: real HTTP is not required to catch this; stub transport + controlled clock exposes resend/return-path bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake clock and stub transport: first call returns rate-limit error, second returns success. Assert original request payload is resent after scheduled delay and `call()` resolves with second response without exposing retry internals.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: requires retry-attempt counter + terminal error-mapping path in the coordinator, not just a standalone constructor check. PASS2 counter-argument (Unit) rejected because behavior text depends on end-of-chain state after multiple attempts, a module interaction failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Stub transport to always return rate-limit responses; run through configured max retries and assert rejection is specifically `QuotaExhaustedError` with expected typed fields, not raw transport/generic error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: this is state-machine correctness across retry coordinator + quota tracker + backoff scheduler. Unit is too low because infinite-loop risk often appears in cross-component transition conditions. PASS2 counter-argument (System) rejected: termination bug is reproducible with in-process fake clock/transport; no real OS/network dependency is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Drive full chain with deterministic fake clock and always-rate-limited transport; assert bounded number of attempts and final error emission occur within expected virtual time/attempt count, with no extra retries.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration brake checks passed: no real infra, no AI/ML dependency, no multi-agentic sequence.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: failure appears when multiple caller paths share state through quota manager; this is module/state handoff under concurrency, not single-function logic. PASS2 counter-argument (System) rejected because behavior text scopes to same process and can be reproduced with in-process async tasks without real network.
behaviors.test_description: Create several concurrent `call()` invocations against one client instance (or documented shared manager) with stubbed transport; assert quota consumption is global/shared and limits are enforced across callers collectively.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: FIFO dispatch is a queueing/orchestration property across scheduler + request pipeline; unit-testing queue helper alone can miss integration ordering regressions. PASS2 counter-argument (System) rejected: real network/subprocess not required; controlled async scheduling with stub transport catches ordering faults.
behaviors.test_description: Enqueue multiple calls while quota prevents immediate send, then release capacity stepwise; assert dispatch timestamps/order match submission order (FIFO) end-to-end through client pipeline.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: No higher-level disqualifiers: not OS/network-bound, no AI output dependency, no multi-agentic chain.
